local_setup_divergence.notes: The isolated Integrations clone had no local Factory context file, so the Bot product context pin 0d972754750c45d73aae4bf3606af248034e8dbf and its pinned Factory objects were used explicitly. The locally modified bic-resolve-rfq skill was recorded as non-Factory provenance and is not committed or used as a runtime dependency.

state_refinement.release_state: Before Bot-bound evidence delivery, Integrations now validates an exact Bot-owned protocol version, schema digest, contract fingerprint, endpoint, and receipt requirement. A mismatch is quarantined before POST and cannot advance the source cursor.
invariants: Protocol mismatch performs no evidence POST and cannot advance a connector cursor.
Integrations never automatically downgrades to the legacy Bot route.
The Bot manifest, schema, and fingerprint remain canonical; Integrations owns only a consumer pin.
Compatibility reports, CI dispatches, and terminal receipts are validation witnesses only.
Canonical evidence, candidate, review, signoff, compliance, and Decision authority remain Bot-owned.

rejected_or_collapsed_states: Version, schema, fingerprint, route, and receipt-policy mismatches share one quarantine state with bounded reason codes.
A successful capability check was not treated as evidence acceptance or Product authority.
minimization_review.rationale: Only a compatible delivery gate and a no-cursor-advance quarantine branch are needed locally; all contract coordinates remain data and evidence rather than new Product lifecycle states.

end_of_session_capture.notes: Integrations #261 and PR #262 contain the producer implementation, pin, CI receiver, and release checklist. Terminal real-process and deployed validation remain downstream in Factory #282.
